This stage is the TUI’s bottom-edge interaction layer: it runs during the main event loop and owns everything the user types, selects, or confirms in the bottom pane, from the persistent chat composer to temporary popups and modal request flows. `mod.rs` hosts that pane, routes keystrokes to the active view, and composes the composer, footer, status surfaces, and stacked overlays through the shared `BottomPaneView` contract in `bottom_pane_view.rs`.

At its core, `chat_composer.rs` builds on `textarea.rs` and `textarea/vim.rs` to provide rich editing, with draft, footer, popup, and attachment state split into focused state modules. It integrates paste detection (`paste_burst.rs`), history recall and Ctrl+R search, slash-command parsing (`prompt_args.rs`, `slash_command.rs`, `slash_commands.rs`, `slash_input.rs`, `command_popup.rs`), and mention/file lookup via plugin inventory, live file search, and the unified `mentions_v2` popup. Older specialized popups like `skill_popup.rs` and `file_search_popup.rs` support that same inline completion role.

The rest of the stage supplies reusable bottom-pane UI machinery—`list_selection_view.rs`, `multi_select_picker.rs`, and the public `composer_input.rs` wrapper—and concrete modal flows for settings, skills, memories, hooks, feedback, custom prompts, app-link guidance, MCP elicitation, and request-user-input rendering.
